channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: You will love long enough to see Japan turn into a brothel for wealthy americans/westerners... — 75 replies — 300.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
You will love long enough to see Japan turn into a brothel for wealthy americans/westerners...

Open thread • Replies: 75 • Updated: 2026-07-01 23:03 UTC

OP

You will love long enough to see Japan turn into a brothel for wealthy americans/westerners...

Uhhh maybe the future isn't so bad at all, I like JP girls.

Early replies (first 3)

62431882
Its already like that. Kind of offputting really.

62431889
>>62431877
Should I have learned Japanese instead of Spanish?
I guess Japanese are more fake and xenophobic compared to Hispanics.

62431901
>>62431889
who gives a shit, you got money. That the point.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62432758
>>62432755
yes, I used to feel pain from cheating too

if you have enough sex you stop caring about women and it doesn't hurt if she cheats

maybe it's jaded.
but no woman is worth feeling bad over. Even the really pretty ones and really nice ones are still whores that cheat. Just like men.

62432768
>>62431877
I just want to find one who will donate eggs so I can have a whole herd of japa-hapa babies via IVF/surrogates.

62432769
>>62432755
also it's just sex

I know you feel love from sex but it's just sex

some other guy's dick doesn't change love just like you fucking some other girl's pussy won't change your love.

jealousy is about owning someone, and that's not love.

62432788
>>62432769
That is the nature intended outcome, we just leverage on it not going against it like what the society has mislead us into, you know getting betraying the entire life make a person fuming flame deep inside them to a point they willing to cross the line to do anything to another person. Say get into heavy debt due to cheating.

62432792
>>62432788
I made my first million dollars to spite my ex

I made my second third and fourth million dollars to spite my ex

take that rage and channel it into useful revenge

62432795
>>62432792
>take that rage and channel it into useful revenge
but be aware, when you get your revenge it won't satisfy. There's no pussy deep enough to bury your hate, and there's no money big enough to buy back your innocence

62432797
>>62432717
>I can pay for sex any time I want. Last time I went to Cabo I had whores lined up to hug me at the all-inclusive resort club.
>the fact is I don't have to pay, your gf's and wives and sisters and mothers are lining up to have sex with me.

62432801
>>62432795
wounded men can do great and terrible things

but that doesn't fix us. It's just a stone in the path

ultimately you lose the ability to love and you will miss it

62432803
>>62432792
There is plenty of romance but their man can only dream of, and the best part is they are free of charge, it is given to another man with no string attached. Beat me.

62432808
>>62432797
I'm much older than that and my chin is much bigger.

here you go, this is me

62432819
>>62431977
>Mexicans get mad at you for speaking Spanish
Is this true

62432863
>>62432819
Sí, porque se te da fatal su jerga ala verga

62432884
>>62432808
>322

where are you in that pic?

62432891
>>62431911
You are a gaijin though? And no japanese people dont see you differently youre still a baka gaijin to them

62432895
>>62432884
I'm the wrinkly viejo pon the lef han side

62432900
>>62432863
Then dont speak English, you're terrible at it

62432904
>>62432900
I'm a native born english speaker with a linguistic IQ of 162.

I speak 5 languages fluently, Neuvo is one of them.

62432907
>>62432904

62432915
>>62432907
just looking for my homies

yos chollos no publican

62432919
>>62431911
those cockasians always ruin everything, sticking their albino neanderthal monkey paws all over everything and fuck it. can't stand them sucking up all the clean air and contaminating everything, giving everyone std's and small pox

62432924
>>62432919
weird how we never had a medium pox

62432925
>>62432915
グーグルが大便利ですね!

62432939
>>62432925
you got me there

only moon runes I know are these

和楽器バンド / 千本桜

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=K_xTet06SUo

62433118
>>62432904
162 IQ, okay what's your net worth?

62433235
>>62431877
Never heard of the homeless girls in Tokyo?

62433263
>>62432722
>it only takes me about 3 minutes to cum
a premature ejaculator who like the smell of his own farts kek

62433266
>>62431877
Party is over. Gaijins are being kicked out.

62436502
crazy that the waifus are getting fucked down by Chinese uncs with big bellies for ez entertainment

62436587
>>62431977
>The moment you start speaking nekkie tongue they get flabbergasted and clap
Nonsense. They continue to treat you like a toddler and ignore any Japanese word that you speak.

62436641
>>62431877
Nothing ever happens. They will be fine. We will get more anime that way.

item.title: >if my 22 year old son is still living at home , I don’t him paying rent, I want him OUT — 24 replies — 96.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>if my 22 year old son is still living at home , I don’t him paying rent, I want him OUT

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-07-01 22:54 UTC

OP

>if my 22 year old son is still living at home , I don’t him paying rent, I want him OUT

Early replies (first 3)

62433628
But saaaaaar isnt this normal in india and all civilized places? Only in the so called united states of america they kick their kids out

62433637
>>62433628
anything that isnt jewish is indian because.... because it just is, okay!!!!

62433641
All you need is a half million dollar down payment and you can get your mortgage payment down to $4,250 per month on your loan against a million dollar house. Then you can live in the desert on top of your neighbors with trees that will fall over during the first rains and slight breeze because they don't belong in a desert.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62433652
>>62433626
Why did he upgrade to such an ambitious microphone? Recording an album? Burning money for no reason?

62434021
>>62433626
how much of a shit job did you do raising your kid if you want them out so young?

62434079
>>62433641
I like how you're pretending that's a starter home on the internet naively thinking nobody will notice your bullshit.

62434100
>>62433641
he probably bought his son one of these, nevermind just covering the down payment on it

rich boomers like this are carrying their chlidren through life. he forgot to mention he paid the rent for his son to move out, then bought him a house.

62434109
>>62433626
>How to ensure you get the niggerest “carers” in the cheapest nursing home and none of your children visit

62435117
>>62433626
I'm still at home and paying rent to my parents at 28. There's no reason to move out. I saved up 100k on my measly 63k/year wage since I started to work 3 years ago and made 10k from that on the market. Only retarded, lead poisoned scum kick their children out if there's nothing wrong with them. It's the most obvious psyop out there meant to please narcissistic boomers and force young people into debt.

62435187
Boomers want to legalize thousandth trimester abortions.

62435219
>>62435187
Nothing wrong with that if the baby is brown

62435449
>>62434079
starter homes are a retarded meme anyway

62435510
>>62434100
He paid for all his children's college tuition and housing. He's admitted in on his show. All the "advice" he gives is contrary to everything he's done himself to get ahead

62435715
>>62435510
how is not getting his kids into debt contrary to his advice of not getting into debt?

62435721
>>62433626
My parents supported me until I was 28. Now I’m a cpa making $150k and own a house and have 3 kids. Who knows where I’d be if they kicked me out at 22. Probably dead.

62435864
>>62435721
Well my family is rich but I was kicked out at 17 when I graduated early and I refused to pay rent(they wanted the going rate in my CA city)
My filling fell out 3 weeks ago and I need a root canal. But I just became a dad so all my money goes toward that. My first kid died a few years ago so, I am trying to be optimistic. I am 34

62435880
>>62433626
>I don’t him paying rent

62435903
>>62434079
(((starter homes))) are a made up buzz phrase to psyop people into reading lying jewish news articles about (((real estate))) and the (((housing market)))

62435925
>>62433626
Normal or rich people don’t do this. A 22 year old is like 1-2 year college.

62436306
>>62435715
his repeatedly told callers that their adult children shouldn't be living with them and that they should be paying rent. He didn't charge his kids rent

62436453
>>62433652
you seen it here first folks, newest rapper on the block dropping the heat

62436581
>>62435510
Boomers, and some older GenX (like Dave), really are out of touch when it comes to the importance of parental assistance.
The era of bootstrap-pulling is long over, and any mildly observant millennial or zoomer has probably noticed by now that their successful peers almost always got help (and a lot of it) from mom and dad.
And it's never been easier to notice this, given how over-exposed people are, thanks to social media, wikip etc.

62436586
>>62435864
Your family can’t be that rich. There’s just no way they’d kick you out at 17 if they were. It’s just not something actually rich people do.

62436615
>>62436306
his callers arent financially stable people whose lives are going well lmao

item.title: Chainlink: PAY UP — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chainlink: PAY UP

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-07-01 22:55 UTC

OP

Chainlink says: the buck stops here. Its time to pay up, druggies.

We won.

Early replies (first 3)

62436310
yep, Sergey and the company will be making a lot of money...to bad the token still remains unneeded and worthless, lol

62436313
You gotta pay up
They gotta pay up
Fudders cannot believe it
Your link bill is past due, unpaid no service it is true
Who is the cuck now who is the chad now you had six years how did you lose?

62436399
>>62436294
I'm tired and stupid, explain please?

Free link services ending? Fees paid for in link, purchased from the market at the 10% premium, or paid in link direct?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62436460
>>62436399
Poopoo peepee

62436489
>>62436294
These funds are going right into the hands of stakers

In two more weeks

62436544
>>62436489
Stakers will get whatever's left after the HR roasties get their rightful raise and bonuses

62436554
Gonna be fun when they’re offering millions for your wallet with early staker privileges.
Kek fuddies.

62436593
>>62436294
>All services denoted in us dollars
Nigga this is just a normal ass software as a service company.

62436610
>>62436593
Yes
> nigga this is just a normal ass software as a service company.
It is? Explain.

62436621
>>62436460
That didn't clarify anything you dork

item.title: I don't even know what my job is anymore. — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I don't even know what my job is anymore.

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-07-01 23:03 UTC

OP

>Be me
>29
>worked at company for 4 years
>Started off in IT operations
>After 2 years reorg changed my role into a service management analyst
>Doesn't really make sense but it will soon
>"trust me bro" - manager (who's empire is collapsing)
>doesn't work
>2 years later new department director
>Knows it's all BS and disempowers us to even do it
>Now I have nothing to do at all
>Now my manager scrambles for new work for us to do
>Just comes up with new thing
>Basically he's trying to justify his own existence because he's down to 5 staff, which is the minimum needed at this company

I'm extremely demoralised. Has this happened to anyone else? I don't even know what job I can get elsewhere because I've spent so long at this retarded company doing fake work hoping it would start to make sense and it never does

Early replies (first 3)

62436031
>>62436009
I work in a factory, with no AC, lost all of my overtime and my shift has gone from 18 workers down to 7 (other 11 were laid-off or fired).

62436037
>>62436031
Rough.

62436040
>>62436009
get out right fucking now if you know what's good for you

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62436055
if you haven't achieved a significant promotion (at least 40-50% salary increase) within 1-2 years of starting an entry level role (judging by the title this is an entry level role or close to it), you have been relegated to entry level hell and your salary adjusted for inflation will forever match what it was when you started, no matter how many lateral "promotions" they give you, until you're fired

speaking from experience as someone who has been promoted 4 times since starting an entry level role in 2020 and now managing the seniors that originally interviewed me

62436088
>>62436009
ive seen this many times in corporate offices. this is why you change companies every 3 years max. not only for the salary increase, but the skill increase. using your instance for example. imagine day 1 you are dropped into a new company and they ask you to partition and encrypt a drive. on paper you have 4 years of experience. but could you do it?

62436120
>>62436037
Oh, but my point in that response was, I've been doing the same factory wagie job for 15 years. Anytime I look at other jobs, they all pay less. Some even require a degree and pay less.

I haven't gained any news skills (except economic, finance, psychology and philosophy education from listening to podcasts and reading for 8 - 12 hours a day, 5 days a week), which are useless at acquiring a job. I've been offered higher roles, but the pay is shit. They offered me.a 50 cent raise to become the shift manager. I laughed in their faces.

62436629
>>62436055
Doesn't apply to me

>>62436040
Why? If they want to make me redundant they'd likely have to pay me about £20K because of the way my corporation is, and unions and employment legislation

>>62436088
Nope, I basically have no skills. Wasted my time here. You're right.

62436643
>>62436629
>brit
You should still apply for better jobs. Eventually your job will close your department and find a reason to fire you legally. The longer you wait the worse it will be.
I recommend moving to the USA economic zone.

item.title: LET'S FUCKING GOOOO — 11 replies — 22.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
LET'S FUCKING GOOOO

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-07-01 22:36 UTC

OP

LET'S FUCKING GOOOO

Early replies (first 3)

62435458
gz

62435459
>>62435387
fake email

62435472
>>62435387
>>62435458
>>62435459
Sir please kindly, have you resolved the issue sir?

What you need is a blockchain expert that can help you retrieve the funds sir.

Have you heard about Mis Petunia Rose Tulips? She has helped me turn my 0.3 BTC into 2.8 BTC. She has been a blessing to me and my family.

Do you have t3l.eg.r4m sir? Please kindly do the needful and install Teamviewer to help her assist you sir.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62435480
>>62435472
Wow <i'm surprised someone else here also knows miss Petunia Rose Tulips. I started with only $5000 and she has turned it into $17000 overnight. It really works!

62435533
>>62435480
I cant believe how good it worked. I only needed to connect the bot to my wallet and within days she made me so much money.

62436108
>>62435387
My portfolio breached 200k today.

62436129
>>62436108
>Screeching fuddie noises

62436130
>>62435472
>t3l.eg.r4m
is that word filtered?
telegram

62436143
>>62435472
Woah 9x roi, your village must be proud of you saar. I had a similar experience but she told me to smear cow dung across my face or else the trades would not go through, was this your experience too?

62436163
>>62435472
Profitable trading requires experience, a solid strategy, and preparation, which we lack. Understanding buy/sell ratios, identifying market leaders, and diversifying are key to reducing risk.
I've Been following miss Petunia Rose Tulips for a while now, and what stand out is how clearly she explains investing. No hype, no pressure-just real guidance that helps people think long term and make a smarter decisions.

62436564
>>62436130
The exchange jews do not want buyers/sellers to P2P via the chat/PMs on their platforms since it would bypass their fees, so they filter word any contact infos and channels.

item.title: VOO and Chill — 177 replies — 364.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 177 • Updated: 2026-07-01 22:36 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62426144
If I'm just starting out with this stuff, what's wrong with 70% VTI / 30% VXUS?

62426154
Honestly I’m seeing a lot of upside to going into QQQ over VOO as long as you are okay with the fact it is more volatile
The upside it’s just so huge when compounded

62426173
>>62426154
Or honestly, as someone already in VOO, I wonder if it makes sense to just go into something like VGT which has consistently beaten VOO and QQQ (for now anyway) or something like DRAM
A lot of VGTs holdings are currently down but it’s YTD is higher

62426183
>>62407078
I keep half of my funds in VTWAX, rebalancing annually. Right now I'm down 0.84% this year, my portfolio is fucked but VTWAX is my safety net.

62427417
I am also curious about DRAM can anyone tell me if it’s a good hold for a short while

62428031
jfc DRAM (ie a thematic ETF) is bullshit made for retard retail investors that get their financial advice from grifters off of tiktok. this happens every time a finance topic hits the news and every time people get burned.
barely worth saying. if you're unironically taking advice from /biz/ you're already doomed intellectually.

62428054
>>62428031
but why exactly, what reason is there to believe the companies within it won't continue to turn a profit for the foreseeable future?

62428080
>>62428031
>>62428054
Like I'm serious, what exactly is wrong about holding it and selling once memory cools down, is it smarter to just go into a more volatile ETF that at least has a longer history if you want more returns, is it just hype and not really the next big thing, explain why

62428238
>>62428080
this is basic finance 101, there are plenty of resources online or off that will explain it to you.
ignorance isn't even the issue here, your reaction to not knowing something is to have random retards on a thinly veiled gambling board tell you what to think. and don't just jump on another grifter selling a course or ask some AI slop engine and ask it leading questions like "why is investing in DRAM a good idea?".

62428337
>>62428238
In other words you don't know

62429683
>>62400493
Anon there is nothing wrong with doing VOO and chill if you have time and principal
Going into wildly volatile ETFs can be okay if it’s a satellite account
Just be careful of FOMO and not being risk averse
You sound like you have a good head on your shoulders though
Remember this is money, real money, your money

62430693
VOO anons where you at?

62430902
>>62400493
Contrarian nerds always lose.
Always.
In middle school dating.
Being high school incels.
In college.
At work.
In love.
At investing.
At all times.
Follow the crowd and try to fit in. Everyone doing anything else is a reject loser.

62431131
>>62430902
Who is being the contrarian here though

62431374
VOO is managed by 2 girl bosses, Michelle Louie & Aurélie Denis. So with your 0.03% management fee you're essentially paying for those do-nothing "day in the life of a portfolio manager" videos for daughters of elite sociopaths.

62431402
>>62431374
A small price to pay for a low expense ratio

62431920
>>62431131
Anyone trying to beat VOO over decades.

62431922
>>62431920
Feel like VUG has consistently beaten VOO though

62431971
>>62431922
Contrarian.
I own VUG and don't own VOO tho :)

62432023
>>62431920
fair enough
thoughts on a younger person doing QQQM?

62434054
Anyone have thoughts or realized returns and experiences on VOO + satellite
accounts instead of just VOO

62435372
Was gonna try to go into this DRAM dip but decided to just do more VOO
But idk a higher return ETF still sounds promising and like a no brainer whether it’s SOXX or QQQM but QQQ has so much crossover with VOO it’s just redundant and only good for growth seasons
I guess this is why VOO and Chill exists to just stop thinking

62436303
>>62431920
What about QQQ though

62436357
i boughted my daily VOO
i'll keep dealing the real world like a good goyim in the mean time.

62436478
>>62436357
huzzah brother

62436499
>>62400493
Where my VGT bros at?

62436507
>>62400636
>Yes and I'm sure that used car and maybe a holiday you can buy with it when you're 65 from cashing out all your investments will be great.....
You say this as if the average /biz/ shitcoin gambler is performing better than VOO which is definitely not the case.

62436510
>>62428031
Your IQ is so actually low. Holy shit.

62436538
>>62436510
Do you think DRAM has value, honest question, and for how long - if so, what dip should one wait for
>>62436499
VGT and QQQ are only doing better thanks to tech having such strong performances for years now, correct?
VOO seems safe since you don't know the future, but on the other hand, thinking the future of the market for the next decades wont always be tech focused seems so short sighted it's silly.....maybe I should start DCA into VGT?

62436562
>>62400493
>VOO
it's EVOO for me lads

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 195 replies — 116.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 195 • Updated: 2026-07-01 21:25 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62419390
go all in hbar when it bottoms at 0.03

62419964
Bump, just woke up, let's get started

62420874
It would appear that everyone is talking about the new WENDYs Stock

Naturally we should take some time to consider this potential moonshot. Could be a waste of time. In a few I will post information I find relevant to the stock and whether investing is the right option

62422903
Another page 9 bump

62423064
Good to see the thread is still going strong. Today is my off day, let's look into the next subject "predictive programming"

We'll start with Oliver tree

62425537
op was not a fag this thread has good vibes we should keep it going until we all make it
personally im trading perps with a system after 10 years of learning charts off/on but finally committing
did a 32x in 2021 and should have been able to retire but didnt anticipate the biden years so here we are going pure psycho mode like we did back then but even harder until money is /solved/, then I'll keep trading for the love of the game cuz it's the best sport in the world

62425849
>>62423064
This shit is way too schizo. No anon you can not predict the future. You can maybe foresee some type of market trends, seasonality or whatever but this is just ridiculous

62425890
>>62425537
Got any good tips/ressources on learning to trade charts? trying to get into it too

62427228
>>62425849
I concede. I'll look for a more productive venture but I won't let the thread or general die, it will be some time before value is struck, I will admit that. I can be tenacious if nothing else.

62428130
>>62425890
emperorBTC's telegram has a basic bitch tldr set of material to learn from. also has a youtube where he goes over stuff. that's where I started and I like that he focuses on the absolute basics, although he leans heavily towards confirmation w/ volume which others may disagree with. btc focused but btc/crypto is perhaps the most effective use of TA.
basically there's no need to get fancy and the chart is the final source of truth because what the price does > what people cope and/or seethe about
support/resistance levels and price action with risk management (defined r:r) = compound as you get gud
once you commit you'll realize "the catch" on people actually make money from trading is is simply how retarded the overwhelming majority of people are that they simply cannot manage a few simple tenets to click buttons and make money, you likely being one of them if you don't develop a process and risk too much on trades
gl anon

62428141
>>62428130
also perps have exploded so trading equities for crypto natives has been a pleasure to avoid tradfi gatekeeping bs
follow the right people on twatter with alerts set to on and it's like having multiple research firms giving alpha for free-- key word "right" accounts. hopefully you have the pattern recognition to figure out which are spewing paid channel larps and which are providing genuine insight
that's basically the whole game. if you can get into a group chat with other chads that's even better because a network is stronger than you alone, although this is def an arena where it ultimately comes down to you mastering yourself (you can't copy trade your way to wealth but you can learn what others do/how they enter/exit positions)
legend trade is a new perps platform that's gamifying this if you wanna start there instead of hyperliquid (front-end using hyperliquid)
Also bigtrout300 on twitter and join his tg as well he's pretty dope. There's a lot of great accounts posting good shit right now. Some really good posters fade in/out of cycles while the scammers/extractors are always abundant, but just use discretion

62428170
>pic related
>"...focus on the chart as these information is very likely to be priced in already"
the "TA is astrology for men" line is used to make fun of retards who take it too far and treat it like divination when in reality it's all stacking probabilities/confluence in your favor as best you can, and from the above example you can see relying on your feed for information will almost always be slower than the chart

62428488
Finally some good posts

62428503
>>62428141
Big trout doesn't post on Twitter and his TG hasn't had activity since May. Any other suggestions?

62428780
>>62428503
His tg is BigTroutTrade and he posts on twitter all the time wdym

62428797
>>62428780
BigTrout Trading on youtube as well

other X goats that have been killing it IMO recently:
@MoneyPrinter0x
and my personal favorite currently:
@Wild_Randomness

there's tons of alpha just follow those people, then see what they repost, then follow the people they interact with that provide research, and even or especially follow the chinese/korean/japanese accounts that post comprehensive semiconductor/other market effort posts since translation is seamless now
never been a better time to go full NEET

62429207
>>62428797
But you're still under $10k?

62429246
>>62378965
Anon Belgium is literally doing very well in the world cup, you should make more of these predictions

62431586
bump.

62431599
probably nothing
bio protocol desci ai BIO now cookin with open ai, nvidia, anthropic, claude news check twita

62432055
>>62373752
Hey
I just started out in this crypto thing and bought $450 worth of solana. How do I become cool and multiply it

62432088
>>62432055
Be like me and listen to people within a group of other retards that are pushing a token. The last one tanked a $200 investment -98% but then he re-deployed the same token and it yielded a 40x from where it was called.

62432306
>>62432088
pardon my incompetence but I didn't understand much of what you just wrote anon

62432350
>>62428797
I had bad experiences following twitter people and waiting for signals even those that seem smart. It can be interesting but you’ll be lost if they randomly stop posting, they’re not funds giving you alpha but people that should have better things to do than post if their full job is actually trading.

I do still like @donalt and @cryptocred their show technical roundup is a rare non clickbaity show that just focuses on TA but most people are inherently frauds on social media

62432360
>>62432055
I don’t think Solana is a good buy right now it’s barely holding support and likely going lower to like 40 if I remember correctly

>>62432088
If you have a group like that be a hero to the community and share it to us

62432373
>>62432360
>be a hero to the community and share it to us
see
>>62385756
I recently held an airdrop for /biz/ too

https://warosu.org/biz/thread/62424715

62434369
Bump, I'm getting an early weekend because of the country's birthday. Will devote time to this thread.

62435112
>>62432350
skill issue. donalt? lol
you gotta follow the small accounts anon

62436077
>>62435112
Why would you rely on random accounts calls and not build the skill yourself? You are just fucked if you only listen to others, and you have no way to know they aren’t just lucky for a while until they drag you down with themselves

>donalt
Yes he may be old and washed but you can still learn a lot from two traders that never shilled some memecoin. Except for ftx but I guess everyone had an ftx sponsorship for a while

62436265
>UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD
>200 REPLIES

LMAOOOOOOOOOOOOOO OH NO NO NO NO AAAAAAAHHHHHH

item.title: WE are the oil marines and we're not fucking selling — 32 replies — 31.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
WE are the oil marines and we're not fucking selling

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-07-01 22:04 UTC

OP

Another cargo ship just got blown up in the strait. They can't keep oil down forever.

Early replies (first 3)

62411834
>it’s true

62412180
I slurped some more oil

62413818
>we are the oil baggies

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62413900
you can sell now at 70
or at 40 when the Ukraine Russia war ends
your choice

62416450
>>62413900
Only a matter of time before reality hits the market. The emergency reserves are not infinite and it will take years to repair the damages in the gulf and in Russia.

62416754
>>62416450
They are actually 1 week away from losing pressure. Iran knows this and is in no rush.

62416764
>>62416450
This is doomer bullshit.

62417845
>>62416764
Look up direct quotes from gulf states about the amount of damage and how long it will take to go back to normal production. Even without the destruction, just the fact that they had to halt production means it will take months to reactivate facilities.
But who am I kidding, you won't look up shit because you are a stubborn low iq peak dunning kruger ass who won't change his mind anyway.

62417869
Oil reserves are the lowest that they have been in ~40 years. Even Sleepy Joe was chewing through reserves to suppress prices and that was without the turmoil in Iran.

The reserves have to be replenished. Even if the conflict ends tomorrow without any more turbulence, the damage has already been done. Some people buy a small position in oil as a hedge for their tech investments. If anything, I have a small position in tech as a hedge and most of my money in oil. Everyone will be crying come September and I will be sitting on fat stacks of cash courtesy of black gold while reminding them that I warned them.

62417932
Sell those bags when you get the chance, strikes happening now but idk how much more people will care

62417947
>>62416450
>it will take years to repair the damages in Russia.
So far, it has taken Russia less than a week to repair crude export terminals. Crude oil export volumes have not been affected.

Refinery damage is harder to see, however it's not nearly as much as the damage in the Gulf, because 50kg warheads simply do not cause much damage

62417949
>>62411825
The price is the price. It reflects a consensus amongst oil traders who are very sophisticated. Why do you think you possess knowledge that they don't? I think some oil stocks can be a good investment but derivatives on the commodities or ETFs that invest in them are a gamble.

62418308
>>62411825
The war hasnt ended and the "ceasefire" is fake and gay

expect $160 a barrel

62418377
Sure, you have the facts on your side. The bears have every world leader on their side, every industrial titan, trillions of dollars of economic output that all depends on oil staying affordable. You're a fool to challenge their ability to continue denying reality, they'll do whatever it takes to keep the numbers healthy.

62420386
>>62418377
That is literally what are those people already doing to keep the price under $100. After a certain point though, they won't be able to hold on anymore.

62421774
>>62413818
>oil barrelies

62421795
>>62416450
>he thinks the real reserves are openly communicated and updated

62423372
>>62421795
Educate me about those super secret subterranean 10 dimensional reserves

62423378
>>62411825
Everytime we have these outsized moves on some random FOTM bullshit e.g GME/BBBY/LUNC/Oil a bunch of you retards develop an emotional attachment to it instead of just trading it and leaving when the attention moves on

62426824
>>62423378
$150 EOY

62428877
>>62411825
How do I invest in oil? I've already filled up large metal tank (40 gallons) with oil.

62428910
I would like to believe the people saying that there are actual supply issues. After all, there's evidence the oil reserves are running out and there are far fewer ships getting out of the strait.

On the other hand I'm a little puzzled at how much the oil price is dropping. Price per barrel is now at pre-war levels, as if nothing has happened (which is definitely not true).

It's like the oil commodity trading market is fake and gay, so when the fakest and gayest faggot of them all (Orange King Migger) speaks, they manipulate the price to whatever he wants. How long can this last, or will being fake and gay work out in the end?

62429038
>>62428910
You need to divorce your 3rd world worshipping 5th columnist world view from your investing strategy. You're more than welcome to worship SneakO and think America is a failed nation running on fumes and that the west will totally fall anyday now but that just isn't a good investing strategy.

62429107
>>62429038
I don't know who sneako is besides the fact that he lives rent free in your head. Thanks for telling me about that.

62431556
>>62411825
How did that thread survife a week?

62431619
>>62411825
You are like the silver baggies saying not selling at $100+

62431639
>>62411825
Sold my short at that 72,7 dip an hour ago and got a long there instead.

62431673
Which oil ETF should I invest into? Tired of tech after finally offloading my MU bags.

62433003
>>62431673
My preference is ETFs based on oil stocks instead of ones that invest in the commodity. I think XOP is a good one if you think WTI will go up as it's based on the producers.

62433007
>>62411825
Price... oh and.. chart

62436326
>>62433007
My USOY bags are printing money and I keep buying into the dip. Only a matter of time before we breach $100 again.

62436431
>>62436326
Hard to say what the fuck is gonna happen. China still has a lot of reserves to release and... I guess Russia exists at the end of the day? Reality will catch up to them eventually but for now it seems like Trump posting and Vance talking decides the prices.

item.title: There is one and ONLY one actual currency in the world: deep creampies inside of attractive women. — 53 replies — 19.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
There is one and ONLY one actual currency in the world: deep creampies inside of attractive women.

Open thread • Replies: 53 • Updated: 2026-07-01 20:18 UTC

OP

There is one and ONLY one actual currency in the world: deep creampies inside of attractive women. This is what everyone wants, what literally the entire economy was built to acquire. Every skyscraper you see was designed and erected for the sole purpose of achieving the aforementioned creampie experience. Every other currency is a proxy for the real one. Gold, USD, Bitcoin, none of it has "real" value. Only the bath of pleasure that comes from nutting in a pretty woman does. Orient your investment philosophy around this fact sooner rather than later.

Early replies (first 3)

62425808
>>62425803
Ok saaar, just sold my pregnantbutt for pretty girl puss!

62425825
>>62425803
Dude creampie feel the best when you are the 18yo not post wall.

62425847
>>62425803
you are an r-selected retard who cannot think 30 seconds ahead

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62426770
>>62426414
Good because there's a lot more of them coming.

62426869
>>62425803
She isn't attractive

62426885
Actually no, this is the mind of a coombrained goyslave and why you will never accomplish anything.

62428162
>>62426885
>accomplish

Ahh there's that cope again. Anything to distract you from your deepest biological urges.

62428259
don't listen, link marines. just buy more link, don't think about this, you are not allowed

62428264
>>62425803
I literally just want to be rich so I can live my childhood summers forever. I don't give a fuck about women anymore. I figured out the key to my happiness and its the in the first two sentences.

62428338
>>62425803
Epstein Islanders would have to disagree on the best currency but you are close just a few years off

62428404
>>62428264
giga cope

62428410
>>62426198
why are they couvering their noise in this manner

62428448
>>62428404
kek no, not even a little bit. Maybe when you hit your 30s and actually have experience with women you'll feel the same. I can see how missing out on "teenage love" would fuck you over with regret forever though. So, maybe you'll never know peace but I've been fucking since I was 14 so I'm doing well. 99% of men who say what I say are probably coping though.

62429586
>tfw have only nutted in 1 girl's pussy in years
Having a wife is... okay.

62429590
>>62429586
I'm 32 virgin

62429603
>>62429590
BAHAHAHAHAHAHAH

62429699
>>62425803
came to a revelation. gay niggas disprove this theory

62429730
>>62425803
Based, the final redpill
>>62426198
A massive boon

62429743
>>62426187
This hits hard.

62429810
>>62429586
It's infinitely better than one night stands

62429930
>>62425803
TRVKE

62430097
>>62425803
cant creampie without commodities though what society lets you creampie without being a tool or part of the machine tell me

62430656
>>62426263
The best example of that is this: https://www.reddit.com/r/DecidingToBeBetter/comments/1uig6mr/happy_marriage_constant_fomo_and_thoughts_about/
You absolute fucktard. Just sexually cheat on your wife with prime puss. It means nothing, it's not your marriage. If no one but you finds out literally no harm is being done.

62430708
>>62430656
prime puss dont play that game. wake up kumar

62430732
>>62429810
If we're solely talking about sex, depends on the ONS
Sex with someone you love is generally better than casual sex, but I've had some pretty above average one night stands

62430847
>>62430656
Fuck you, that redditor too has the wrong idea. Either divorce her if the love is gone or quit being retarded. You can literally go to all these places with your wife and make love to her there.
t. khhv

62431534
>>62426290
I've tried making my goal to orgasm to as many of the prettiest girls I could without paying for sex and I genuinely didn't feel any better.
I looksmaxxed, optimized every dating profile and cold approach etc.
After the orgasm I felt I wasted a ridiculous amount of time for a not so fulfilling experience 9/10 of the time. Many times I even felt like a sociopath, just efficiently optimizing every interaction to sleep with the girl.
I think I'm far happier just committing to a single loyal nice cute girl, regularly seeing my family/friends and focusing on my hobbies. It's just a way better effort to reward ratio.

>>62426885
>>62428162
nah this anon is right. Working towards something you believe in will grant the most satisfaction in total.

>>62429810
>>62430732
Yeah, some ONS are decent if the chick is really enthusiastic about being your slut, but it's just easier having your regular girl that knows the positions you like, your kinks, how long to suck you off, when to use the lube, what to say/when to be quiet, sticking her tongue out to catch the prize. Plus you don't stress about whether she's ridden in STDs etc.
The only times I've really liked ONS is when the chick was really pretty but reserved, then flips to be a complete slut when it's bedroom time. It's more of a psychological victory than a sensual enjoyment. Maybe it's healthy to get some of those under your belt, but after a point it's just too much effort to keep trying to hit that same high again.

62431703
>>62430097
>You can't creampie unless there is a marketplace for commodities
What did he mean by this?

62431714
>>62425803
I'm 26 and have never had sex/kissed.

62432347
I am a 27 years old khhv

62435558
>>62425803
OP, you might be onto something. But have you considered the next best thing: Virtual Sex? Sure, it's not as good. But it still captures some fraction of the value, is infinitely replicable, and the experience is getting better every year...

62435582
>>62431534
Consider legal prostitution jurisdictions. Ordering sex should be like ordering a nice meal from a restaurant menu where you can see what you will be enjoying for the evening. Here is HK (priced in HKD not USD)

62435994
>>62428338
Marie Rose is peak value (she's 18)

item.title: Micron and DRAM — 54 replies — 18.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Micron and DRAM

Open thread • Replies: 54 • Updated: 2026-07-01 20:06 UTC

OP

While obviously no one can predict the future perfectly, it seems foolish to not invest in something related to AI RAM / HBM Production for the next few years as contracts are already made and supply is bottlenecking
Given what I say is true, which would you guys say is a better choice to start pumping my paychecks into (I already have $60,000 in a market ETF and want more upside)
For some reason my gut is siding towards DRAM, still heavily concentrated but at least has some diversification
It seems like those top 3 companies in it are just making more and more supply deals
Additionally, every piece of evidence I read suggests memory will bottleneck for another year or two
Is it smart to just start DCA into DRAM and then sell once I’ve had my fill (not expecting a perfect pull out timing!)
Again I just wanna do this rather than simply doing stuff into VOO or VGT or something
How much of this is hype and how much of this is brain dead free money that will beat the market
Please be serious and honest

Early replies (first 3)

62427339
>>62427323
you will be eaten mark my words. most of these companies have massive software bloat on their ai's and you wont even get a good exit when all these contracts get put on hold indef while they operate on half of what they are already using and beating metrics by 2 or 3x. the tendency of programming efficency will take your ass out. there will be no warning and demand will cease.

62427365
>>62427339
Is the demand not coming from a need though? At first it was GPUs but now everything doesn’t have enough memory to communicate at the necessary level

62427441
>>62427323
that's a 4 at best

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62430446
>>62430354
go long software. the contrarian goes long on hardware. its not a safe bet. all that shit has to be changed out much faster than it did in the early 2000s. it gets thrown into a shipping container and shipped to a somolian trash pile to be sorted out and burned in a pot for the metals. it looks fucking great right now, people rode the upside of the arbitage between the time it takes them to adopt their own methods. each one of these companies is gonna adopt the apple mentality but it will be with chipsets and manufacturing. we used to live in a world where it came from china or a little island with the blueprints. that is not the case anymore. the hardware isnt even the biggest cost for the AI giants.

62430659
>>62427323

62432266
.

62432668
>>62427323
Semiconductor funds have been crazy profitable and seem to only be increasing. The real money is in selling dumbass AI companies the hardware for their ultimately doomed datacenter investments.

62432824
>>62432668
Do you think Semis > Memory?

62432833
Semis will crash hard once China catches up. Not interesting in that ticking time bomb

62432934
>>62432833
Is memory a safe bet then
DRAM

62433058
>>62430446
>go long on the sector that is still dumping
>don't invest in picks and shovels sector that has no end in sight and has insane earnings reports one after another
I wouldn't listen to this guy.

62433271
>>62427323
Obviously, smart people invested in this in 2024, or last year at the latest. Now is the time to sell, obviously. How is that not obvious to you? Are you a shill? Are there SHILLS and BAGHOLDERS on 4chan?

62433313
>>62432824
>>62432934
Look at these questions made of pure retail last leg.

62433362
>>62427323
rumor of new architecture causing fear for the memory final solution
careful with your longs anons

62433597
>>62433313
What makes you say this

62433722
>>62427339
>you will be eaten mark my words. most of these companies have massive software bloat on their ai's
??????????? The hell are you talking about ABSOLUTE retard? DRAM companies are about manufacturing RAM sticks.

62433745
you fucking cunts /smg/ has been telling you to buy Micron for MONTHS now and you morons are still holding on to your crypto bullshit

62433773
>>62427323
>How much of this is hype and how much of this is brain dead free money that will beat the market
30/70 imo. When you understand the tech underlying this and its actual implementations in every industry, you start to understand that it's genuinely not a bubble, it's the biggest productivity increase since computing but is happening at a much faster rate than computer adoption. If anything the markets are currently UNDERvaluing these stocks because retail investors (and smaller institutions) can't understand the tech.

Memory companies are a decent mid-term hold but after a couple years we will see a simultaneous drop in demand and increase in supply and their margins will collapse back to normal levels - I plan to divest in 12-18 months.

Long term I foresee Anthropic and especially OpenAI being the big winners. Like, 90s Apple levels of winning. These two have an iron grip on flagship LLM tech and frankly can charge whatever the fuck they want for enterprise-level applications and flagship API access. Google and X are the closest to matching them but both are 1-2 years behind if we're being generous. Chinks (and non-Americans in general) are so far behind it's comical. Certain big tech companies (notably Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta, not necessarily in that order) are also poised to win big thanks to their adoption and utilization strategies; Microsoft had a big league but has kind of dropped the ball, but I don't think any of those 3 are going to be the big success that OpenAI/Anthropic will be.

TSMC and NVDA are both excellent long term holds, NVDA arguably moreso (and I say this while being someone who thinks the chances of a CCP invasion, ever, are ~0) but both are great, if a bit overbought in the short term. When I divest from MU etc, TSMC and NVDA are where the cash is most likely to end up.

62433786
Unironically
If someone wanted more safety is SOXX a better bet? I know it’s Semi Conductor focused and not RAM
It is still up triple digits percentage wise over the last year and has a longer track record of 25 years
Is it a smart investment for an overall AI Satellite account versus something thematic like DRAM? Also considering BAKA but don’t know much about it
This is for someone investing over time, short or medium term is fine, I just mean not a giant lump sum

62433799
>>62433773
Okay your claim about betting on Memory through 2027 (12-18 months) feels in line with everything I’ve read
What do you plan on doing to get exposure to memory just something like DRAM or a different option
I’m fine with most of my long term holds just being broad ETFs instead of only betting on tech, but meanwhile short to medium I’m unsure if I take more risk

62433801
>>62433786
I'm not really sure since not all semiconductor companies are created equal. A lot of them are specialized in ways that aren't conducive to AI and while they're completely fine as long-term stable investments, that's not why you or anyone else is investing in SOXX. Personally I'd pick and choose.

62433818
>>62433799
I'm pretty basic and generally more risk averse than other tech bulls so I just sank a bunch of money into MU, WDC, STX, and SNDK, no leverage. If MU hits 1600 I might sell since that's being cited as a potential ceiling by a large number of analysts and frankly the sentiment is more important than any technical validity in this time frame imo. Also fwiw VGT is the second largest position in my Roth IRA but I view that as a 10+ year hold.

62433837
>>62433818
I just have a giant share of VOO from DCA my paychecks into it without thinking but kinda felt like that was wasting a big opportunity now
Hard to say it without is sounding like FOMO cause it’s not
Just looking for a smart hold that should beat the market average that I can sell and then just circle it all back into VOO and chill, I’m nearing 6 figures in it as is which is my goal for this year

62434663
.

62434778
DRAM Down 10% today
Is this a buy?
Trading at $67 currently

62434788
it's over, memory is gay and bankrupt

62434794
>>62434788
You say as Micron just signed another deal this time with GM

62434941
>>62430659
Same.

62435104
>>62433362
i tried to warn you...

62435154
>>62434778
I am also curious
Is this a buy or maybe wait for 20% down

62435463
>>62435154
Eh whatever just bought more voo

62435926
Yeah fuck this shit i gave it some thought and realized it makes no fucking sense unless you have a ton of principal

item.title: Theyre ending the Chainlink BUILD program — 126 replies — 15.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Theyre ending the Chainlink BUILD program

Open thread • Replies: 126 • Updated: 2026-07-01 15:07 UTC

OP

LMAO!!!!!

https://chain.link/blog/build-program-evolution

Early replies (first 3)

62417511
xrp is the chosen coin…it’s undeniable at this point

62417512
Come on man, this shit still spamming after 8 years? Just give it up. There are thousands of other coins.

62417514
"Chainlink Labs is transitioning from payments made in project tokens to focusing on commercial agreements involving fees paid in LINK or other liquid assets that can be readily converted to LINK."

Build members will buy my bags?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62424386
>>62422668
It literally says "the most recent season of Chainlink Rewards will mark the conclusion of Build-related token rewards, with claims ending on July 7, 2026" meaning no season 2+ of build rewards going to stakers.

It also says "Proceeds from these new agreements are expected to be programmatically converted to LINK and used to support the growth of the network through programs such as the Chainlink Reserve." meaning that link goes to the reserve, not to stakers.

Previously
>stakers will get build rewards

Now
>Chainlink gets paid in link

62424528
xswap isnt even on cow swap? where do you sell this trash?

62424728
S E X

62424821
>>62424386
Brutal

62425620
>>62424023
>this project has always been pushed by morons
>also I've been holding this project for years

You're lying. And brown.

>>62424386
You're a brown ESL monkey who doesn't even know what the words "such as" mean.
Protip: the same exact words were used when Build was first introduced.

62425675
>>62417503
HAHAHAHAHAHAHA Another nail in the coffin and shovel full of dirt in the grave on the biggest retail fraud in token history.

62425681
>>62419638
Link is underperforming fucking death spiraled LUNC since Trump revived crypto. Lol And by a lot.

62425687
>>62419707
>A chainlink shill calling someone else brown.

62425699
>>62422159
>>62423276
Perfectly said.

62425962
>>62425620
I'm a 100k link holder. I have more link than you.

"Such as" isn't very fucking reliable. All of this would be fine if link was valued as it should be but it fucking isn't, with no clear path to gaining that value.

62425980
>>62425962
>CLL uses "such as" in 2022 when launching Build
>THIS IS SET IN STONE

>CLL uses "such as" in 2026 when changing Build
>THIS IS NOT VERY FUCKING RELIABLE

You're a brown paid fuddie.

62426004
>>62425980
I'm also whiter than you, Yank.

62426010
>>62425980
>>62425620
post a picture of your hand with timestamp

62426949
i am just getting caught up on this news after the weekend. my my my how the mighty have fallen

i predicted all of this for the last few years but the boyscouts couldn't handle it

>duuuuuuuuuuuude i need a team of tax attorneys for the heckin rewards

BAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHHAHAHAH

look at you now. an expensive lesson on controlling your ego. you needed it tho


your hubris was your downfall

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62426967
>>62417512
>There are thousands of other coins.

LOL this dude stuck in 2021. that's your conclusion bro, just buy other scam coins?

don't you people get it? altcoins are dead. capital moved back to actual companies in the stock market. only a retarded sap would continue donating to a bunch of nerds for a "whitepaper"


>>62417833
>I'll also (again just playing the other side) point out the regardless of whether or not it ultimately benefits the LINK token, Chainlink is realistically one of like 5 crypto projects amongst the hundreds of "legitimate" ones that actually is seeing real adoption and has a huge use case that is surviving the infantile stages of crypto, and not just surviving but will thrive. So again, who knows if this ultimately helps the token price, but I do understand why linkies hold onto some faith still.

so are you just a fan of the company?

>>62417873
>It’s all push and pull. Ying yang. If you have 20 million earning you 800k (500k post tax) per year

who the fuck would do that? the $20m has drawn down 80% so no, life is not good. anyone not retarded would've parked most of that in the s&p500 and has been making at least 15% per year

if you really wanted pathetic 4% return you could get that in a savings account where your only risk is that actual bank going bust

62427007
>>62418846
> The opportunity to invest in something like Chainlink - core infrastructure with insane value capture potential, has never really existed before. The closest thing maybe would be investing in AMZN prior to cloud services,

you are not invested in chainlink. you made a donation kid

amazon's revenue in year 7 was $3B. and that's in 2001 numbers. you can go ahead and stop comparing to your shitcoin bro you're embarrassing yourself

>>62420893
>The fuck is that analogy lmao. This isn't a casino you dumb faggot, real people invested real money into an asset that promised certain things by certain dates and they have not only underdelivered but are starting to actively ignore those promises.

it's the end stage of a cult investment where they turn on each other. it somehow becomes the fault of the "community" on why they didnt make any money.

in order to preserve their ego, guys like >>62420825 have to spin the story that you'd have to be crazy to expect any kind of gains. mind you the rest of the world's market all hit all time highs while you are down 80-90%. by creating this narrative, he avoids recognizing he's holding a really bad investment and can continue on thinking he's a secret genius.

62427022
remember this old pasta? still relevant today. every cl product roughly follows this life cycle

62429766
>>62425681
Oh I remember this one - this poster is an actual bot.

62431562
>>62419888
The best part is SIR GAY NIGGEROV got all these companies to be part of the BUILD program, and then those companies did actually in many cases issue tokens but most did not provide tokens to CLL to give out as rewards for participating in the Staking pool. If there had actually been airdrops from every single participant then the program would have brought some relevant value to token holders instead of what, like 9 companies giving tokens long after their tokens had already followed the shitcoin chart to its completion and become totally worthless.

62431631
soon the staking program will be canceled too
and then stinkies will be out of all hope
feels good

62432093
>>62431631
Well the new line seems to be the price will improve when all link is circulating and the team has fully divested itself of the token. Which i don't know if it's just late stage imbecile drivel or the most brazen shill line ever attempted in history.

62432101
>>62425620
I was making Teagan memes at the san fran meet up in 2019. I honestly wish it was otherwise but I've wasted fucking years with link.

62432489
>>62417503
Bullish for LINK

62433501
Why are the fuddies always the ones with the most posts?

62433680
>>62423312
First of all there are no vacations until the wife gives you kids.

62433701
>>62427022
checked and /thread

62434455
>>62433501
its what they're paid for, the number of posts

62434545
>>62433501
>>62434455

It’s obvious that they are paid to do this; what I don’t get is whether they’re doing it to get midwit like me to buy (and I bought without regrets), or to get dumbs to sell.

62434643
>>62434545

62434699
>>62433501
>>62434545
>blatant samefagging
why are shillers such grifting and disingenuous retards?

item.title: sony is stopping disc releases for PS games you know what that means — 30 replies — 11.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
sony is stopping disc releases for PS games you know what that means

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-07-01 20:28 UTC

OP

sony is stopping disc releases for PS games
you know what that means

Early replies (first 3)

62434070
Believe it or not -- calls.
(/v/ermin might disagree)

62434082
>>62434061
So they're using GTA as the 800 lb gorilla to strong arm this through.

There's literally no reason not to abandon consoles completely and go full PC at this point. And I was a diehard Sonybro a few years ago. Then one day a lightbulb clicked, no new gens to buy, no backwards compatibility issues, I can play all my games in one place, with superior graphics, forever.

Yeah fuck this noise.

62434586
>>62434061
Every company:
>how can we gradually make our products worse?
Why though?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62434618
>own nothing

62434620
>>62434061
Does it mean that SSDs are going to be more expensive?

Because if you don't know already, most of the playstation games are already "code discs", which means they don't even include the game and you can't play it without downloading a patch and has been like this for years.

62434693
>>62434061
Seems like scraping the barrel for savings. Might boost some interner provider's numbers a bit? I dunno.

62434712
>>62434586
when DEI retards are in management, they always do this, i never saw any manager focus on making product better, they always want to make it cheaper

62434713
short GME

62434731
>>62434061
unironically invest in physical discs

rare ps5 games, ideally ones taken down from store too (but they already pump fast)

62434737
>>62434731
like that deadpool ps4 one they lost rights too, easy 10x in price

62434748
>>62434061
>you know what that means
OH MY GLORP! THEY GOT RID OF THE HECKING DISCOPOPS!

62434831
>>62434620
yes

62434840
>>62434061
This is actually bullish. This means less cost to manufacture discs

62434843
>>62434082
Fuck yeah bro let's switch to PC! All of those games have physical discs and all towers have disc drives!

62434864
>>62434712
It's not DEI management you retard. It's literally capitalism and the need to increase profits every quarter. Browns have nothing to do with this.

62434884
>>62434061
Lmao who the fuck still buys vidya in 2026. You have piracy. There's about 10,000 solid vidya to play over the past 40 years. Download an emulator and get on it faggots

62434892
>>62434082
get all your roms and emulators and back it up and keep one offline emulator PC/laptop for the future.

62434906
>>62434737
>just baghold the shitty $50 game for 10+ years to have that 1 greasy mystery meat mf trying to negrotiate it down to treedfiddy bro

62435107
>>62434731
I threw away like 2 grand of ps3 game discs because I just don't care

62435164
>>62434864
That's not capitalism tho, that's gov't regulation

62435169
Online games are easier to monetize. It's simple economics. Look how much money GTA online has made in the past decade selling DLC to kids.

62435290
>>62435107
rip

62435322
>>62435164
No anon. Government regulations are a good thing and help the consumer. Do you not remember when Apple was forced to drop their proprietary charging cables in favor of USB-C? That was government regulations that did that. Or don't tell me that you'd rather be forced to get new proprietary charging cables with every new phone you got?

62435373
>>62435322
Government regulation is literally what forces companies to disregard investing in their employees and the long term in favor of chasing higher quarterly profits for their investors, which is what that post was a complaint about.

62435392
>>62435373
Goverments USED to do that when they actually had taxes in the 70s and earlier, all the extra declared profits would just get taxed so the companies were forced to either spend it on themselves or give it away, and they spent it on themselves through reinvestment and employees.
Then Reagan happened and "trickle down economics" was sold to the masses that if we just cut regulations, businesses will still spend on themselves instead of chasing profits. Cutting regulations led to heaps of shitty bad loans being spread out through the mortgage market causing the 08 collapse lol.
You're a clown.
Lol.

62435404
>>62434061
> trends in consumer preference

62435437
>>62434061
Consoles and gaymes on disc media is ancient games slop, nigga for goodness sake Stream released on pc like 25 years ago
I haven't used physical media installs since the fucking early 2000s

games don't fit, updates don't fit, nothing fits

physical media is pure shit for games

early consoles with cartridges and when games fit on a single cdrom was a different era

>mah I wont own anything!
fuck off physical copies if it's a real game, if it's live content games that can be pulled because it's on servers I don't care it's slop anyway, just play it enjoy the experience then realize life is futile and nothnigs lasts forever

62435625
>>62434843
No dickhead, but if there's no disc anyway why the fuck not just switch to PC?

62435679
>>62435437
Ok schlomo

62436053
>>62434061
Sony:
>invent multiple iterations of laser disc technology
Also Sony:
>abandon all forms of laser disc technology

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62432880
>>62432820
slap and run, what a pussy

62433022
>>62432820
average stinky linky vs boomer

62433046
that shit is staged right...like some faggot got his cousin to slap him perfectly filmed and made a gofundme while the assailant "got away" and everyone clapped

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62433070
>>62433046
based einstein poster

62433075
>>62432820
total wagie death

62433088
>>62433046
>and everyone clapped
but I didn't clap..

62433210
>>62432820

62433222
>>62433210
>Huffy bikes
they gave him the cheapest $150 bike lol

62436224
>>62433210

62436237
Dance, wagie.

62436253
>>62436237
DO THE WAGIE

62436266
It was a wage... the wagie dance...
They clocked right in without a chance.
They packed their lunch in Tupperware,
Then smiled and said, "Good morning, Claire."

The boss walked in, "Need one more task."
The wagie sighed behind his mask.
He worked till six, then worked till nine...
The overtime was "family time."

62436287
>>62432820
>dude's already halfway out the door before his hand even touches his face
What a pussy. Terrified of potential repercussions.

62436312
Clean up on aisle who gives a fuck.

62436338
HELP
I'M BEGGING YA

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 52 replies — 11.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-07-01 18:23 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62429392
>>62426552
Because we're asking. What's wrong??

62429688
We will keep holding

62429720
>>62427357
You really know what you're talking about

62429723
Not buying but riding out the 'storm'. There have been much worse times.

62430182
>>62429723
Ride well

62430234
need to sell enough today to pay rent and bills

62430266
>>62430234
Don't sell

62431888
>>62429688
You can always hold, or stake it if you want to earn a bit while waiting.

62431895
>>62426626
depends on which country for sure

62431900
>>62426512
I go in at $100K. Every $20 I spend BTC drops another $1,000.

62431909
My plan is, quite simply, not to own shitcoin

62431916
>>62431909
Like memecoins???!!!

62431991
>>62431888
not possible
>>62431900
Spend every day

62432200
Thoughts on ansem everyone

62433067
>>62426733
Btc is memecoin? Wow

62433595
>>62431991
It's possible. Babylon already supports native BTC staking, if you haven't seen it yet.

62433598
>>62426638
We'll see how it plays out. Good luck either way.

62433616
The elites are finished with Bitcoin. It has served their purpose.

62433621
>>62433616
is bobo based on Shane Gillis? I can't unsee it.

62433746
>>62433595
is this real?

62433751
>>62433616
you know nothing at all

62433844
>>62433598
Back at ya anon. Wishing you much WAGMI

62433854
>>62426512
I'm not retarded so I'm never buying BTC for any price. Being a cryptotard in 2026 is even more pathetic than being a /pmg/tard

62433884
>>62433854
Your lack of retardness coupled with no faith in yourself is why you will never WAGMI

62433961
>>62426512
I don't think about BTC. It was created by centralized exchanges to attack Bitcoin. You're only talking about it because they printed a bunch of tether to get the price to $100k. There are no users, only a small group of poor speculators paying actual USD for this coin. The rest of it is tether iou's.

62434984
>>62433746
Yeah, it's real. Go look into it if you're skeptical. They even have their TBVs in public testnet now.

62434990
>>62433844
Twins?! Who

62434999
>>62433844
Twins?! Who's your bet? Lol

62435029
>>62433961
If there were really no users, I don't think we'd still be seeing institutions, ETFs, and developers building around Bitcoin.

62435427
>>62433884
Why do you like sending beauty women
